name: Santo Maris Oia Luxury Suites & Spa

content.verdict: Santo Maris is Oia's largest proper luxury resort — 58 suites and private-pool villas set on the inland (non-caldera) side of Oia. You trade the sunset-cliff view for several things cliff-front hotels can't offer: a full-scale spa pavilion, multiple restaurants, three outdoor pools, real grounds to walk, and significantly better value. Rooms and villas are spacious, immaculately kept, and most mid-tier categories include a private plunge pool. For honeymooners who prioritize spa treatments, pool time, and gardens over the direct caldera drop — and who'd rather spend their savings on excursions, dinners, and wine — Santo Maris is the smart pick.
content.best_room: Santo Maris Villa with Private Pool — a one-bedroom villa with private heated pool, sundeck, and garden. Spacious and more private than the suite categories. The Honeymoon Suite with outdoor jacuzzi is a more compact, budget-friendlier option.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Transfer, welcome at the villa, dinner at one of the on-site restaurants
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer, welcome at the villa, dinner at one of the on-site restaurants.

itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast, full spa day (Santorini's largest spa pavilion — use it properly).

itinerary_7_nights.description: 10-minute walk into Oia for the sunset; linger for dinner at Ambrosia.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Winery tour with driver (Santo Wines and Domaine Sigalas)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Winery tour with driver (Santo Wines and Domaine Sigalas).

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach day at Ammoudi, Red Beach, or Perissa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Beach day at Ammoudi, Red Beach, or Perissa.

caveats: No direct caldera view from the hotel — you walk 10 minutes into Oia to see the sunset (which is what everyone does anyway).
The hotel is larger and more resort-like than most Santorini properties; if you want intimate boutique feel, look elsewhere.
Families do stay here, though couples dominate.
Some rooms are further from the main pool/spa — confirm location when booking.

faqs.q: Does Santo Maris have a caldera view?
faqs.a: No — it's set on the inland/Aegean side of Oia. For sunset, walk 10 minutes to the caldera edge. Many guests prefer this arrangement.
No, families are allowed. The atmosphere is mixed but leans adult.

faqs.q: Why choose this over a cliff-front hotel?
faqs.a: Better spa, bigger rooms, private-pool villas, on-site restaurants, and 40-60% lower rates than equivalent-category cliff-front properties.
It's the largest proper spa in Oia — hammam, multiple treatment rooms, couples' suite. Genuinely a reason to book the hotel.

faqs.q: How far to the main caldera path?
faqs.a: About 8-12 minutes on foot depending on your room location.
A Villa with Private Pool or a Honeymoon Suite with Outdoor Jacuzzi, depending on budget.
